name: Mock HTTP Client Framework
description: Implement a mock HTTP client framework across three modules. Module http_types.py must define: HttpMethod enum with values GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH; Header dataclass with fields name (str), value (str); HttpRequest dataclass with fields method (HttpMethod), url (str), headers (list[Header], default empty), body (any, default None), query_params (dict, default empty); HttpResponse dataclass with fields status_code (int), body (any, default None), headers (list[Header], default empty). Module mock_server.py imports from http_types.py and implements MockServer class with: register_route(method, path, handler) where handler receives HttpRequest and returns HttpResponse; register_middleware(middleware_func) where middleware_func takes (request, next_handler) and returns HttpResponse; handle_request(request) -> HttpResponse that matches route, runs middleware, returns 404 if unmatched and 405 if method not allowed; path parameter support with :param syntax. Module client.py imports from http_types.py and mock_server.py and implements HttpClient class with: constructor taking MockServer; set_base_url(url); add_default_header(name, value); get/post/put/delete methods taking (path, **kwargs) supporting headers, body, query_params and returning HttpResponse.
test_imports: from http_types import HttpMethod, HttpRequest, HttpResponse, Header
signature:
# http_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any

class HttpMethod(Enum):
    GET = 'GET'
    POST = 'POST'
    PUT = 'PUT'
    DELETE = 'DELETE'
    PATCH = 'PATCH'

@dataclass
class Header:
    name: str
    value: str

@dataclass
class HttpRequest:
    method: HttpMethod
    url: str
    headers: list[Header] = field(default_factory=list)
    body: Any = None
    query_params: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
    params: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)

@dataclass
class HttpResponse:
    status_code: int
    body: Any = None
    headers: list[Header] = field(default_factory=list)

# mock_server.py
from http_types import HttpRequest, HttpResponse, HttpMethod
from typing import Callable

class MockServer:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def register_route(self, method: HttpMethod, path: str, handler: Callable) -> None: ...
    def register_middleware(self, middleware: Callable) -> None: ...
    def handle_request(self, request: HttpRequest) -> HttpResponse: ...

# client.py
from http_types import HttpMethod, HttpRequest, HttpResponse, Header
from mock_server import MockServer

class HttpClient:
    def __init__(self, server: MockServer): ...
    def set_base_url(self, url: str) -> None: ...
    def add_default_header(self, name: str, value: str) -> None: ...
    def get(self, path: str, **kwargs) -> HttpResponse: ...
    def post(self, path: str, **kwargs) -> HttpResponse: ...
    def put(self, path: str, **kwargs) -> HttpResponse: ...
    def delete(self, path: str, **kwargs) -> HttpResponse: ...
tests:
def test_basic_get():
    server = MockServer()
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.GET, '/hello', lambda req: HttpResponse(200, 'Hello World'))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    resp = client.get('/hello')
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.body == 'Hello World'

def test_post_with_body():
    server = MockServer()
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.POST, '/data', lambda req: HttpResponse(201, req.body))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    resp = client.post('/data', body={'key': 'value'})
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert resp.body == {'key': 'value'}

def test_path_parameters():
    server = MockServer()
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.GET, '/users/:id', lambda req: HttpResponse(200, f"User {req.params['id']}"))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    resp = client.get('/users/42')
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.body == 'User 42'

def test_default_headers():
    server = MockServer()
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.GET, '/check', lambda req: HttpResponse(200, {h.name: h.value for h in req.headers}))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    client.add_default_header('Authorization', 'Bearer token123')
    resp = client.get('/check')
    assert resp.body['Authorization'] == 'Bearer token123'

def test_middleware():
    server = MockServer()
    log = []
    def logging_mw(request, next_handler):
        log.append(f'{request.method.value} {request.url}')
        return next_handler(request)
    server.register_middleware(logging_mw)
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.GET, '/test', lambda req: HttpResponse(200, 'ok'))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    client.get('/test')
    assert log == ['GET /test']

def test_404_not_found():
    server = MockServer()
    client = HttpClient(server)
    resp = client.get('/nonexistent')
    assert resp.status_code == 404

def test_405_method_not_allowed():
    server = MockServer()
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.GET, '/only-get', lambda req: HttpResponse(200, 'ok'))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    resp = client.post('/only-get')
    assert resp.status_code == 405

def test_base_url():
    server = MockServer()
    server.register_route(HttpMethod.GET, '/api/v1/items', lambda req: HttpResponse(200, 'items'))
    client = HttpClient(server)
    client.set_base_url('/api/v1')
    resp = client.get('/items')
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.body == 'items'
constraints: MockServer returns 404 for unmatched paths, 405 for wrong method on existing path
Middleware functions receive (request, next_handler) and return HttpResponse
HttpClient.set_base_url prepends base URL to all request paths
Default headers are included in every request from the client
Client methods support body, headers, query_params keyword arguments
http_types.py exports HttpMethod, HttpRequest, HttpResponse, Header
mock_server.py imports from http_types.py and exports MockServer
client.py imports from http_types.py and mock_server.py and exports HttpClient
